name: Sarah Myerscough Gallery
recommended_action: TARGET GALLERY. Leading London contemporary craft/collectible design gallery specializing in wood and sculptural design. Participates in PAD London, Design Miami, TEFAF, Masterpiece. Relocating to larger Mayfair space Oct 2025 with new Crafted Art Foundation. DIRECT ALIGNMENT with wood artist goals.
column15: TIER A IMMEDIATE: Research current represented artists. Engage authentically with exhibition posts. Attend physical exhibitions if possible. Build relationship over 3-6 months before direct outreach about representation.

name: TEFAF Maastricht
recommended_action: World's premier art fair for fine art, antiques, and design. 276 dealers from 24 countries. 20th century design section. Sarah Myerscough Gallery exhibits here. Major collector/curator/dealer attendance.
column15: TIER A: Monitor exhibitors and programming. Engage with educational content. Follow galleries that exhibit. Build expertise on TEFAF aesthetic to align work positioning.

name: House & Garden UK
recommended_action: Condé Nast publication. Major UK interior design influence. Features craft, collectible design, architecture. Press coverage here = validation + collector visibility.
column15: TIER A: Study past craft/design features. Identify staff/editors. Engage thoughtfully with posts about wood, craft, sculptural design. Potential PR target once gallery represented.

name: Ingrid Pilkington-Wright
recommended_action: Head of Sales at THE NEW CRAFTMAKER (Pimlico Road, Belgravia). Former Harvey Jones Kitchens. Interior design background. CRITICAL BRIDGE NODE - can introduce to gallery network, collectors, designers.
column15: TIER A IMMEDIATE: Reconnect professionally. Mention shared interest in contemporary craft. Ask about The New Craftmaker program. Request studio visit or coffee. She has direct gallery/collector access.

name: Nicole Hollis
recommended_action: AD Top 100, Elle Décor A-List designer. San Francisco-based (NICOLEHOLLIS Inc). Works with UHNW clients, top galleries. Known for integrating collectible design and craft. PRIME COMMISSION SOURCE.
column15: TIER A: Engage with her posts about craft, materials, sculptural objects. Study her projects for wood integration opportunities. Potential to spec work into luxury residential projects.

name: Cluster Crafts
recommended_action: London-based craft fair platform. Celebrated by Guardian, Forbes, Condé Nast. Features emerging artists across ceramics, glass, furniture. Less established than Sarah Myerscough but good visibility platform.
column15: TIER B: Monitor application cycles for exhibitors. Build portfolio aligned with their aesthetic. Apply when ready. Good stepping stone for early-career visibility.

name: Future Icons
recommended_action: Curated by Louisa Pacifico. Represents design/craft businesses. Future Icons Selects at London Craft Week (May 2026) - 40+ makers, 95% UK-based. Platform for emerging/established makers.
column15: TIER B: Research Louisa Pacifico. Study represented makers. Engage with Future Icons Selects content. Potential application/representation opportunity.

name: Future Icons Selects
recommended_action: Annual London Craft Week exhibition. 83 Rivington Street, Shoreditch. Ceramics, textiles, metalwork, collectible design. Direct to collector sales opportunity.
column15: TIER B: Attend 2026 exhibition (May 14-21). Network with makers and collectors. Study selection criteria for future application.

name: Full Grown
recommended_action: Gavin Munro's tree-grown furniture company. Highly innovative, conceptual approach to wood. 4-8 year growth process. In permanent collection National Museum of Scotland. PEER/COLLABORATOR potential.
column15: TIER B: Study their process and positioning. Engage as fellow wood innovator. Potential collaborative opportunities or shared exhibition contexts.

name: Heather Waugh Pitts
recommended_action: Nova Scotia-based ceramicist. Self-taught, works in porcelain. Interior design background. Nature-inspired. NOT UK-based (Canada), limited strategic value for UK gallery ecosystem.
column15: TIER C: Light engagement only. Geographic mismatch limits strategic value.

name: Kieu Tran
recommended_action: Oakland CA-based ceramic sculptor. Biomorphic abstract/figurative forms. SECA nomination SFMOMA 2024. Represents contemporary craft sculpture positioning. PEER reference for positioning.
column15: TIER B: Study her positioning and career path. Understand how sculptural craft moves into contemporary art contexts. Potential peer network on West Coast.

name: Marcus Rowlands Studio
recommended_action: Likely furniture maker/craftsperson. Limited data found (possible confusion with Marcus Rowan/Novocastrian). Needs more research to confirm identity and strategic value.
column15: TIER C: Research further before engagement strategy.

name: Unknown
recommended_action: Insufficient data to classify. Username suggests craft/ceramics but no public profile found. May be private account or emerging maker.
column15: TIER C: Monitor but low priority without more information.

name: Argentum Studio
recommended_action: Limited data. Likely metalwork/jewelry studio based on name (argentum = silver). Needs enrichment.
column15: TIER C: Research further. Potential peer network if confirmed craft studio.

name: Brooke Litchfield
recommended_action: No clear professional profile found. May be collector, designer, or private individual. Needs enrichment.
column15: TIER C: Low priority without clear professional role.
